text:
So now Michael Bay and Co. are going to take Optimus Prime, the great and noble leader of the Autobots, and the one the most heroic icons and role models for young kids ever, and turn him into a villain. 

Y'know, I really didn't think I could learn to truly HATE and DESPISE Michael Bay anymore than I do now for single-handedly  ruining one of my favorite heroes from my childhood.  But, of course, that self-righteous, egotistical, money-grubbing asshole has proven me wrong again!
author: @thundermaxx1273

text:
Michael bay has ruined transformers so badly. humans were supposed to be the faithful allies and now they are back stabbing scumbags. I agree that is probably closer to truth but keep in mind what are the kids of today going to learn from it growing up? all the justice are gone, all the chivalry are gone. if you don't think that matters to young people, just imagine if snow white in her own fairytale had to have sex with each of the 11 mini men to exchange for food while she lies helpless in bed, whats that going to play in the heads of kids? transformers IS the boy version of fairytale, as 35 year-olds we all grown up watching and looking up to them, that's why bumble bee is a Camaro and not a Lamborghini, because Lamborghini and Ferraris are too far away to reach for a common boy, but Camaros are much closer to dream about, and how in many Michael Bay transformers bumble bee had to bend over and get fucked to make ends meet? 
think about that.

Trust me, when realistic big azz true decepticons come to destroy, i'd like to see any kids say... "I want to stay and fight." One more thing, please quit trying to kill off Optimus, its not dramatic if he keeps coming back. These Transformers movies have so much potential but they are lacking in something. text: "For my world to live, yours must die." NO Optimus's morals have always been the one thing that has kept him good. He's lost Ironhide, Jazz, Ratchet, his entire team. He thinks he destroyed Cybertron when in reality it was the war and Megatron that destroyed the Golden Age. Without his team to reassure him, Optimus has totally gone dark. Plus, I can't have been the only one to notice that in Age of Extinction, Lockdown took Ratchet's spark instead of destroying it. I have so many theories though about this whole movie. Like in G1, they went to war with the Quintessons because the Quints. said that they created the transformers. Optimus's maker sure looked like a more up to date version of G1s Quintessons. And then of course there's the dark energon like they used in TFP. I'm gonna be so pissed of they kill off optimus.

text:
With the AllSpark gone,
We cannot return life to our planet.
And fate has yielded is reward.
A new world to call home.
We live among its people now, hiding in plain sight but watching over them in secret. 
Waiting.. Protecting. 
I have witnessed their capacity for courage. 
And though, we are worlds apart,
Like us,
There's more to them than meets they eye. 
I am Optimus Prime, and I send this message to any surviving autobots taking refuge among the stars. 

We are here. 

We are waiting.

text:
*SPOILER ALERT!!*


Optimus Prime is captured and controlled by the Creators. The Creators promise to restore Cybertron by reforming earth, which will kill mankind in the process, in exchange Prime retrieves them the cybertronian Excalibur sword as it is a key weapon to stop Unicron, the Chaos Bringer who was awakened after eons of slumber due to Cybertron's destruction and is on his way to Earth to destroy the last of the Primes. Cade and his team manage to uncover the Excalibur in the protection of Dragonstorm and Steelbane, the last remaining members of a legion of cybertronian warriors known as the Knights of Cybertron. The T.R.F. hunts down and eliminates the Dinobots, including Grimlock. The Creators arrive and begin attacking earth. Megatron forms an alliance with the humans to save Prime and defeat the Creators. The battle begins. Cade and Bumblebee manage to overpower and release Optimus from the Creators's control. Cade. Optimus and Megatron confront and defeat the Creators. Megatron betrays and battles Optimus for the Excalibur. Major William Lennox sacrifices himself to destroy the Creators's mothership, causing their forces to escape Earth. Megatron kills Optimus but is overwhelmed by earth's forces and escapes. Optimus dies after passing the Matrix of Leadership to Hot Rod, who transforms into Rodimus Prime. In post-credit scene, Unicron devours a planet full of life and sets his course for Earth. The End!

text:
I think there's much more to this film than we might even imagine by the trailer. I believe we get only few glimpses of it. Just look it profoundly, look at how Cade refuses to give up on Prime, look at how Bee, how was like a son to Prime, has the courage to fight him - maybe trying to get the real him back. I don't think Bee will die, I imagine something like this: Bee is just very very damaged and on point of death, only then Prime awakens again and truly see what he is doing. Moreover, I can feel a lot of love coming out of this trailer, in all its shades. Just think of the past movies and then watch this again taking into account my words. 
There's something to it that we can't even imagine by now. 
And I can't wait for June.

text:
Actually, I am a little bit disappointed with this movie. Well, if we look back at the 2007 movie, the movie is more at present, not the future like this is. What I mean is, the movies have gone from old and good  present, to a new and hard future. The only Autobots who is left from 2007-2011 is Optimus and Bumblebee. They others is dead or have leave earth and we probably ever won't see them again. I miss when it was more at 'present' like in 2007, 2009 and 2011. Present. But in the 2014, and now 2017, I feel that they have made the future here little crazy and fast. Nothing old is left. Just new future, new future. They couldn't have made so that some more autobots would have survived? Too late for Jazz, Ironhide, Que, Ratchet and Leadfoot, but maybe Dino? Sideswipe? And Jolt? What happened to him exactly? Was he even in the Dark of the Moon? The twins and the three arcee sisters are also dead. I feeling that if maybe Ratchet wouldn't have to die i Age of Extinction, that he survived, I would probably have been not so disappointed. They killing lovley and old Autobots from the 2007-2011 movies, some survived, but we don't get to see what actually really happened to them? And why would even Hound, Crosshairs and Drift come to Earth only to be hunting down or hide? I really wished that they could have change some things in the movies. Just let one more survive or try to hold on to the present and safe but slowly going over to the past, and not just rush over to it. If something of this would have been changed to, I would be more okay with it than disappointed.

This is what I think and it is my thoughts, so if you think something else or have other thoughts, I will respect it, and I hope you will respect mine. :^)

text:
I absolutely love the Transformers franchise; the music is bone chilling, the action is epic and the CGI is right at the top. Sure there is moments in the movies where it feels like it should be called Humans Fight robots or Explosions Everywhere, but each franchise is different from the others and no movie is perfect. Transformers is a thrill ride for those who grew up with it and a taste of memories for younger audiences. This franchise does get a lot of hate but who can hate Peter Cullen’s Optimus? He’s a role model for not only myself, but many people around the world. The new movie, The Last Knight does look pretty awesome with the look of Cybertron and Optimus having a bigger role to play. There will always be haters but TF fans know and love the movie. So haters, get squashed by Rouge Optimus and let everyone have opinions. But still get squashed 😂😂
“Autobots, roll out!”

text: *Sigh* Not gonna lie, this action looks dope, but even if this ends up being an enjoyable experience the film as whole will suffer because it's a sequel to Transformers AOE. They keep adding new storylines and Mcguffins that don't match up with storylines presented in previous movies. The worst part is that it doesn't matter to the filmmakers because their target demographic are autistic 30 year olds who will pay to see bright colors and explosions  rather than a cohesive story. They should have rebooted it, got rid of Bay, Wahlberg, and all the other lackadaisical humans leads, and tell a story with characters I can care about rather than just another big budget car/toy commercial. Please, for future generations, make movies that are more than just mindless action.

text:
There are two observations in regards to my theory about the plot that I would like to share:
1). How is it that such of an evolved species displays this level of obsession with power and are still going through civil war stages?
2). Then the thought of how obsessed mankind has and still is with immortality, or simply the need to adapt to a hostile reaction of nature
... see where I'm going... so
Here is my theory:
1). Transformer = Human's next evolution step.  Where in a near future, we will figure how to transfer our consciousness to virtually immortal vessels
2). They are us coming from the future, and not aliens from another galaxy.  Then...
3). Earth = Cybertron
(or at least I believe Earth is destined to wear it just like an Exo-suit ;))
...I can't really think of any major flaws when applying my theory to the prequels; besides the fact they haven't mentioned the details of their true origins yet (for obvious reasons)

So here are a couple of things I would like to see by the end:
1). The Witwicky's bloodline evolving into the Prime's legacy (that would be epic) and that is why the remarkable connection Sam had with Prime in the Fallens' storyline mainly
2). What life lesson is the movie (story) going to leave us?  I dare to say this factor will determine its impact to our generation as on how the saga will be remembered and its purpose applied as tool in making us want to be better

text: OK, I had too watch this trailer a couple of times, just so I can talk about this trailer naturally. This trailer looks good, but it isn't amazing, in fact the way they have this made, they spoiled basically everything in the film, granted this film isn't the first contender of doing this, but it's easy to tell what is going to happen in what order. Granted the scenes they chose are good choices, as evidenced by the reaction of people in the comments at things like Optimus ripping off Bee's "wings" or the destruction of the 5-6 cybertronians -I refuse to call them Cybertronian Knights or the Infernicons at this stage- but the rest of the footage, just seems OK. The scene with Quintessa -the "Maker"- was alright, nothing too special. From this trailer I get a bit of a "Dark of the Moon" vibe, mainly with Earth being threatened to be turned into Cybertron. This film looks like it has the same problem as the last films, where it focuses more on humans being a mcguffin for giant robots, as well as having the problem of who is who due to the dull colours, except for Optimus and Bumblebee because they are the -ones who sell toys- most recognizable faces in Transformers. Look, I see this film being an average film, it won't be anything too spectacular, call me a hater all you want, I won't care, I'm not a hardcore fan who hates everything about anything not G1, not am I an average moviegoer, I am just a casual TF fan showing my opinion on a trailer. Now, that I got that out of the way, I would like to say that Bumblebee isn't going to die in this film, so don't worry, he is the kid appeal character, he is the one who sells toys, he is the leader of the Autobots -since Optimus is evil- and he has his own spin off movie coming next year, so yea he isn't going to die.

text: Whether or not it's like the Transformers we remember back in the day, no one can't say that Michael Bay didn't give these movies justice. For those that say the plots of the movies suck, it's not like we said back then that the show/comics suck because of the story. What we remember is vehicles transforming into robots, and this is what these movies bring. You can't tell me that the first time you saw Optimus transform in the first movie, you didn't jizz in your pants. And the action scenes are just so crazy! I believe these movies are PG-13, but if these deaths were in a movie done to humans, it would clearly be rated R. We have faces torn in half and flesh stripped off, with gunshots straight to the friggin face! So no one can't say that these movies aren't done justice. This movie I feel will be the same based on these action scenes. Go Michael Bay!

text: This trailer is actually a lot better at 0.75 times speed. You can actually tell what's going on in every shot instead of it just being quick flashes. It gives just enough time to take in and appreciate all the hard work that went into all this CGI. It builds up the drama more. And even the lines delivered are better paced. Honestly, I wouldn't be surprised if most parts of this trailer were originally at or around that speed, but were then sped up for the trailer. Although, this is Michael Bay. But it goes to show that he could be a good director if he just chilled out and slowed down just a little bit.
